---
title: "Indonesia NCD Simulation Model"
subtitle: "Technical Overview --- CVD and Cancer Markov Frameworks"
author: "RTSL / University of Washington"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  beamer_presentation:
    latex_engine: xelatex
    includes:
      in_header: beamer_preamble.tex
    keep_tex: true
    slide_level: 2
classoption: "aspectratio=169"
bibliography: references.bib
csl: apa.csl
link-citations: true
nocite: |
  @Vollset2024, @ConsortiumRisk2023
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- PART 1 --- PROJECT OVERVIEW                                   -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

# Part 1 --- Project Overview

## Background and Motivation

- Indonesia faces a growing NCD burden driven by population aging, high smoking prevalence, rising obesity, and limited screening access
- CVD and cancer together account for over 50\% of premature mortality in Indonesia
- Policy decisions require quantitative evidence on intervention scale-up trajectories and their expected health and economic returns

\begin{keyinsight}[Why Markov?]
Unlike system dynamics approaches (e.g., NCDSim), discrete-time Markov models allow transparent, state-by-state tracking of disease progression---making calibration to GBD and validation against observed rates straightforward.
\end{keyinsight}

## Aims and Scope

\textbf{Primary aim:} Project health and economic outcomes of NCD policy packages in Indonesia from 2025 to 2050.

\medskip

\textbf{Two independent disease modules:}

1. \textbf{CVD} --- IHD, ischemic stroke, hemorrhagic stroke, hypertensive heart disease
2. \textbf{Cancer} --- 16 types from GBD 2021, in three analytic tiers

\medskip

\textbf{Policy levers modeled:}

- Hypertension control scale-up (HEARTS)
- Sodium reduction
- Trans-fat elimination
- Statin coverage expansion
- Cancer screening (cervical, colorectal, breast)

## Reproducible Research Workflow

\begin{center}
\textbf{Inputs} $\rightarrow$ \textbf{Scenario Definitions} $\rightarrow$ \textbf{Model Outputs} $\rightarrow$ \textbf{Documentation} $\rightarrow$ \textbf{Reports}
\end{center}

\bigskip

| Stage | Source |
|-------|--------|
| Raw data | GBD 2023, UNWPP 2024, IHME, NCD-RisC, ETIHAD |
| Scenarios | `04_define_interventions.R` / `settings.yml` |
| Simulation | Markov engine per module |
| Post-processing | DALYs, VSL/VSLY calculations |
| Reporting | Beamer slides, R Markdown, policy briefs |

\begin{methodsnote}
All scenario definitions are code-driven and version-controlled---no manual spreadsheet steps in the analytic pipeline.
\end{methodsnote}

<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- PART 2 --- TECHNICAL INFRASTRUCTURE                           -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

# Part 2 --- Technical Infrastructure

## Agentic Coding Approach

- Development assisted by Claude Code (Anthropic) for code generation, review, and documentation
- AI-assisted workflow embedded in version control via \texttt{CLAUDE.md} project instructions
- Enables rapid iteration on intervention logic and scenario definitions while maintaining auditability

\begin{keyinsight}
The agentic approach accelerates development of complex modules (e.g., 850+ line HTN control logic) while preserving human oversight through structured code review and calibration checks.
\end{keyinsight}

## Reproducibility Infrastructure

\textbf{What is currently in place:}

- Organized directory layout: \texttt{data/raw}, \texttt{data/interim}, \texttt{data/processed}
- Configuration via \texttt{settings.yml} (cancer) and flag variables (CVD)
- Numbered script execution order (00--08 for CVD; 00--06 for cancer)
- Intermediate \texttt{.rds} checkpoints at each pipeline stage
- Remote function sourcing with local fallback paths

\medskip

\textbf{Not yet in place:}

- No \texttt{renv.lock} --- dependencies installed manually
- No formal seed control for stochastic components
- No containerized environment (Docker)

\begin{cautionbox}[Reproducibility Gap]
Adding \texttt{renv} lockfiles and a \texttt{Dockerfile} would close the main reproducibility gaps. These are planned but not yet implemented.
\end{cautionbox}

## Version Control and GitHub Workflow

- Repository hosted on GitHub under \texttt{Disease-Control-Priorities}
- Branch-based development with pull request reviews
- \texttt{CLAUDE.md} serves as machine-readable project documentation, checked into version control
- Raw data files excluded via \texttt{.gitignore}; processed \texttt{.rds} files tracked selectively

\begin{methodsnote}[Data Governance]
Raw GBD/UNWPP data are not stored in the repository. Analysts must obtain them independently and place them in \texttt{data/raw/}.
\end{methodsnote}

<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- PART 3 --- METHODOLOGICAL OVERVIEW                            -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

# Part 3 --- Methodological Overview

## Full Model Architecture

\begin{center}
\textbf{Population} $\rightarrow$ \textbf{Risk Factors} $\rightarrow$ \textbf{Disease Models} $\rightarrow$ \textbf{Outcomes}
\end{center}

\bigskip

\small
| Component | Status | Key Data Source |
|-----------|--------|-----------------|
| Demography / Population | Scaffolding only | UNWPP 2024 |
| Risk factor distributions | Implemented (CVD) | NCD-RisC, IHME DIET |
| CVD natural history | Fully implemented | GBD 2023 |
| Cancer natural history | Fully implemented | GBD 2021/2023 |
| Health systems / treatment | Partial (coverage params) | ETIHAD, HEARTS |
| Economic evaluation | Fully implemented | Robinson VSL, World Bank GNI |
| Module integration | Separate pipelines | --- |

\normalsize

\begin{keyinsight}
CVD and cancer modules run independently under a conditional independence assumption. No comorbidity interaction is modeled in v1.0.
\end{keyinsight}

## Core Model Logic: CVD

\textbf{State space} (per age / sex / location / cause / year):

$$\text{Susceptible} \rightarrow \text{Incident} \rightarrow \text{Prevalent} \rightarrow \text{Dead}$$

\medskip

- Transition probabilities calibrated to GBD 2023 incidence, prevalence, and mortality
- Background mortality layered from UNWPP 2024 life tables
- Secular trends in case fatality forecast via ARIMA (\texttt{forecast} package)

\begin{methodsnote}
Constraint enforced: $IR + BG_{mx} \leq 1$ for all age--sex--cause--year cells. Violations are flagged in \texttt{032\_adjustments.R} and corrected before simulation.
\end{methodsnote}

## Core Model Logic: Cancer

\textbf{7-state Markov model} (per age / sex / location / cause / year):

$$\text{Well} \rightarrow \text{Precancer} \rightarrow \text{Local} \rightarrow \text{Regional} \rightarrow \text{Distant} \rightarrow \text{Cancer Death}$$

with absorbing background death state.

\medskip

\textbf{Three analytic tiers:}

1. \textbf{Tier 1} (11 cancers) --- Incidence/mortality only (no staging)
2. \textbf{Tier 2} (3 cancers) --- Staged: local, regional, distant
3. \textbf{Tier 3} (2 cancers) --- Precancerous lesions modeled (cervix, CRC)

\begin{methodsnote}
Tier 3 cancers include screening interventions that shift stage distribution at diagnosis, directly affecting transition probabilities.
\end{methodsnote}

## Intervention Logic

\textbf{CVD interventions} are applied multiplicatively to incidence rates:

$$IR_{\text{treated}} = IR_{\text{baseline}} \times (1 - \text{coverage} \times RR_{\text{effect}})$$

\medskip

\textbf{Multi-intervention stacking order:}

1. Sodium $\rightarrow$ BP distribution shift via \texttt{get.bp.prob()} coefficients
2. Statins $\rightarrow$ direct IR reduction (RR from meta-analyses)
3. TFA elimination $\rightarrow$ IR reduction via $RR = 1.28$ per 2\% energy from TFA
4. HTN control $\rightarrow$ ETIHAD-based RR per 10 mmHg BP reduction

\medskip

\textbf{Cancer interventions:} Screening coverage expansion shifts stage distribution at diagnosis, reducing late-stage presentation and improving survival.

## Economic Evaluation Framework

\textbf{Value of Statistical Life (VSL)} approach [@Robinson2019VSL]:

$$VSL_{\text{country}} = VSL_{\text{base}} \times \left(\frac{GNI_{\text{country}}}{GNI_{\text{base}}}\right)^{\varepsilon}$$

\medskip

- Three income elasticity scenarios: $\varepsilon = 1.0, 1.2, 1.5$ (primary: 1.2)
- VSLY derived: $VSLY = VSL / \bar{e}$ (average adult life expectancy)
- GDP growth projections from SSP2 scenario

\begin{takeaway}
Economic valuation converts deaths averted and DALYs prevented into monetary terms, enabling comparison across intervention packages and informing resource allocation decisions.
\end{takeaway}

## How Modules Integrate

\textbf{Current state:} CVD and cancer pipelines run independently, producing separate outputs (deaths averted, DALYs, economic value).

\medskip

\textbf{Integration points:}

- Shared demographic inputs (UNWPP 2024 population, life tables)
- Shared economic framework (VSL/VSLY methodology)
- Common reporting templates (Beamer, R Markdown)

\medskip

\textbf{Not yet integrated:}

- No joint population projection feeding both models
- No comorbidity or competing-risk adjustment across CVD and cancer
- No unified scenario configuration file

\begin{cautionbox}[Integration Pending]
A formal integration layer---shared population module, unified scenario config, and joint output aggregation---is planned for v2.0.
\end{cautionbox}

<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- PART 4 --- DETAILED MODULE SLIDES                             -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

# Part 4 --- CVD Module Walkthrough

## CVD Pipeline Architecture

\small

```
00_run_model.R          (orchestrator + config flags)
  |-- 01_utils.R        (BP control, TFA policy, age grouping)
  |-- 02_load_inputs.R  (sources 021--023)
  |   |-- 021_get_base_rates.R   (GBD 2023 extraction)
  |   |-- 022_get_tps.R          (transition probabilities)
  |   |-- 023_get_tps_bgmx.R     (background mortality trends)
  |-- 03_calibration.R  (sources 031--032)
  |   |-- 031_calibration.R      (calibrate to GBD)
  |   |-- 032_adjustments.R      (IR + BG.mx <= 1)
  |-- 04_define_interventions.R  (scenarios, ~57 KB)
  |-- 05_build_baseline.R        (cohort assembly)
  |-- 06_run_scenarios.R         (simulation engine, ~84 KB)
  |-- 07_output_dalys.R          (YLL, YLD, DALYs)
  |-- 08_economic_value_calculation.R  (VSL/VSLY)
```

\normalsize

\begin{keyinsight}
Configuration flags in \texttt{00\_run\_model.R} allow skipping expensive stages when intermediate \texttt{.rds} files already exist.
\end{keyinsight}

## CVD: Data Loading and Calibration

\textbf{Data sources ingested:}

- GBD 2023: cause-specific mortality, incidence, prevalence (4 CVD causes)
- UNWPP 2024: population by age/sex, life expectancy, fertility
- NCD-RisC: historical blood pressure distributions (1990--2019)
- IHME DIET: sodium intake estimates

\medskip

\textbf{Calibration pipeline:}

1. Extract single-year age rates (20--95) from 5-year GBD groups via interpolation
2. Compute transition probabilities from rate-to-probability conversions
3. Enforce $IR + BG_{mx} \leq 1$ constraint; flag and correct violations
4. Forecast secular trends in case fatality using ARIMA

\begin{methodsnote}
Inline validation: \texttt{print(anyNA(data.out))} after every merge/join in \texttt{021\_get\_base\_rates.R}.
\end{methodsnote}

## CVD: Intervention Definitions

\textbf{Four intervention families} defined in \texttt{04\_define\_interventions.R}:

\small

| Intervention | Scale-up | Target | Key Parameter |
|-------------|----------|--------|---------------|
| Sodium | Linear 2025--2030 | $-15\%$ / $-30\%$ | BP shift coefficients |
| TFA | Linear to 0\% | Elimination | $RR = 1.28$ per 2\% TFA |
| Statins | Linear / logistic to 2050 | 60\% coverage | LDL meta-analysis RRs |
| HTN control | Quadratic (NCD-RisC fit) | 3 scenarios | ETIHAD RRs per 10 mmHg |

\normalsize

\medskip

HTN control is the most complex module (~850 lines), fitting quadratic scale-up functions to historical NCD-RisC data and producing coverage trajectories (Business-as-usual, Progress, Aspirational).

\begin{takeaway}
Output: \texttt{covfxn2.csv} with columns for location, year, coverage scenario, absolute change, and annual rate of change.
\end{takeaway}

## CVD: Simulation Engine

\texttt{06\_run\_scenarios.R} (~84 KB) is the primary simulation engine:

- Iterates over all age/sex/location/cause/year combinations
- Applies intervention effects via coverage-adjusted relative risk reductions
- Supports parallel execution across intervention combinations
- Outputs: deaths averted, incident cases prevented, prevalent cases

\medskip

\textbf{Intervention stacking} applies effects sequentially to avoid double-counting:

1. Sodium $\rightarrow$ shifts BP distribution (population-level)
2. Statins $\rightarrow$ reduces IR directly (individual-level)
3. TFA $\rightarrow$ reduces IR via RR (population-level)
4. HTN control $\rightarrow$ applies ETIHAD RR to treated fraction

\medskip

\textit{Insert figure placeholder: \texttt{output/slides/cvd\_scenario\_comparison.png} --- Deaths averted by intervention package, 2025--2050}

## CVD: DALY and Economic Outputs

\textbf{Burden calculation} (\texttt{07\_output\_dalys.R}):

- YLL = deaths $\times$ residual life expectancy (UNWPP 2024)
- YLD = prevalent cases $\times$ GBD disability weights (cause-specific)
- DALYs = YLL + YLD, aggregated by age, sex, cause, year

\medskip

\textbf{Economic valuation} (\texttt{08\_economic\_value\_calculation.R}, ~42 KB):

- VSL calibrated to Indonesia GNI with income elasticity $\varepsilon = 1.2$
- GDP growth from SSP2 projections (IIASA)
- Outputs: monetized value of deaths averted, DALYs prevented, cost per DALY averted

\medskip

\textit{Insert figure placeholder: \texttt{output/slides/cvd\_dalys\_averted.png} --- Cumulative DALYs averted by scenario}

\textit{Insert figure placeholder: \texttt{output/slides/cvd\_economic\_value.png} --- Economic value of intervention packages}

<!-- ============================================================ -->

# Part 4 (cont.) --- Cancer Module Walkthrough

## Cancer Pipeline Architecture

\small

```
library.R               (setup, loads packages + fnx/)
  |
  |-- fnx/              (23 reusable functions)
  |   |-- project_markov_trace_dt.R  (core projection)
  |   |-- calc_bsln_tps_dt.R        (baseline TPs)
  |   |-- calc_intv_tps.R           (intervention TPs)
  |   |-- calc_metric_dt.R          (GBD extraction)
  |   |-- run_ccpm.R                (cohort component projection)
  |   |-- gen_lifetable.R           (abridged life tables)
  |   |-- process_intv_scen_inputs.R (scenario processing)
  |   +-- [16 other helpers]
  |
  |-- scripts/          (numbered execution)
      |-- 00_read_data.R
      |-- 01_process_country_param.R
      |-- 02_process_bsln_scen.R
      |-- 03_process_pop_proj_bsln_dt.R
      |-- 04_process_intv_scen.R
      |-- 05_process_pop_proj_intv_dt.R
      +-- 06_dissertation_output.R
```

\normalsize

## Cancer: Three-Tier System

\textbf{16 cancer types} organized by analytic complexity:

\medskip

\textbf{Tier 1 --- Incidence/mortality only} (11 types):
\small
Uterine, esophageal, thyroid, stomach, liver, pancreatic, ovarian, bladder, lip/oral, nasopharynx, pharynx
\normalsize

\medskip

\textbf{Tier 2 --- Staged} (3 types):
\small
Lung, prostate, breast (local $\rightarrow$ regional $\rightarrow$ distant)
\normalsize

\medskip

\textbf{Tier 3 --- Precancerous lesions modeled} (2 types):
\small
Cervix, colorectal (well $\rightarrow$ precancer $\rightarrow$ local $\rightarrow$ regional $\rightarrow$ distant)
\normalsize

\begin{keyinsight}
Tier 3 cancers are the primary targets for screening interventions, where early detection shifts stage distribution and reduces late-stage mortality.
\end{keyinsight}

## Cancer: Core Markov Projection

\texttt{project\_markov\_trace\_dt.R} implements the core engine:

- 7$\times$7 transition probability matrix per (location, sex, cause, year, age)
- State vector: \texttt{pwell, pprc, plcl, prgn, pdst, pcdx, pbgdx}
- Forward projection via matrix multiplication, year-by-year
- Two absorbing states: cancer death, background death
- Handles newborn cohort initialization for population projection

\begin{methodsnote}
Transition probabilities are first computed at calibration years, then expanded to the full projection period via \texttt{expand\_tps\_years\_dt.R}. Matrix corrections enforced by \texttt{correct\_markov.R}.
\end{methodsnote}

## Cancer: Key Functions

\small

| Function | Role |
|----------|------|
| \texttt{calc\_bsln\_tps\_dt} | Baseline TPs from stage distributions + GBD rates |
| \texttt{calc\_intv\_tps} | Modify TPs for intervention scenarios |
| \texttt{calc\_metric\_dt} | Extract incidence/prevalence/mortality from GBD |
| \texttt{run\_ccpm} | Cohort component population projection |
| \texttt{gen\_lifetable} | Standard abridged life table ($q_x, l_x, e_x, S_x$) |
| \texttt{process\_intv\_scen\_inputs} | Parse scenario YAML/Excel inputs |
| \texttt{frp\_fnx} | Finite rate projection and RR adjustments |
| \texttt{calc\_start\_hstate\_dist\_dt} | Initial health state distribution |
| \texttt{id\_target\_cc\_methods} | Identify cervical cancer screening methods |

\normalsize

\medskip

\textit{Insert figure placeholder: \texttt{output/slides/cancer\_markov\_trace.png} --- Example Markov trace for cervical cancer (Tier 3)}

## Cancer: Intervention Scenarios

\textbf{Screening interventions} processed by \texttt{process\_intv\_scen\_inputs.R} (~17 KB):

- Define target coverage, efficacy, and ramp-up schedule
- Shift stage distribution at diagnosis (earlier detection)
- Modify transition probabilities accordingly via \texttt{calc\_intv\_tps}

\medskip

\textbf{Population projection} via \texttt{run\_ccpm.R}:

- Cohort component model: aging, births, deaths by age/sex
- Baseline and intervention projections run separately
- Difference yields intervention-attributable outcomes

\medskip

\textit{Insert figure placeholder: \texttt{output/slides/cancer\_stage\_shift.png} --- Stage distribution shift under screening scale-up}

\begin{takeaway}
Cancer outputs mirror the CVD structure: incident cases, deaths, DALYs under baseline vs.\ intervention, enabling consistent cross-module comparison.
\end{takeaway}

<!-- ============================================================ -->

# Part 4 (cont.) --- Pending Modules

## Demography Module

\begin{cautionbox}[Module Pending]
The \texttt{code/demography/} directory exists as a placeholder. No implementation is present.
\end{cautionbox}

\medskip

\textbf{Planned scope:}

- Unified population projection feeding both CVD and cancer modules
- Cohort construction with age/sex-specific mortality, fertility, migration
- Currently, each module handles demographics independently:
    - CVD: loads UNWPP data in \texttt{05\_build\_baseline.R}
    - Cancer: uses \texttt{run\_ccpm.R} for cohort component projection

\medskip

\textbf{Integration benefit:} A shared demography module would ensure consistent population denominators and avoid duplication.

## Shared Utilities

\begin{cautionbox}[Module Pending]
The \texttt{code/utils/} directory exists as a placeholder. No shared utility functions are implemented.
\end{cautionbox}

\medskip

\textbf{Candidate functions for extraction:}

- Rate $\leftrightarrow$ probability converters (currently sourced remotely in cancer module)
- Age-group harmonization (5-year $\leftrightarrow$ single-year)
- GBD data loaders and standardizers
- Common plotting themes and output formatters

## Health Systems Module

\begin{cautionbox}[Module Pending]
No dedicated health systems module exists. Treatment and coverage parameters are embedded in intervention definitions.
\end{cautionbox}

\medskip

\textbf{Current approach:}

- CVD: coverage trajectories defined in \texttt{04\_define\_interventions.R} (quadratic fit to NCD-RisC historical data)
- Cancer: screening coverage targets defined in scenario YAML/Excel inputs

\medskip

\textbf{What a dedicated module could add:}

- Treatment adherence modeling
- Health workforce constraints
- Supply chain / medicine availability
- Facility-level capacity modeling

## Comorbidity and Competing Risks

\begin{cautionbox}[Not Modeled in v1.0]
CVD and cancer modules assume conditional independence. No comorbidity interaction or formal competing-risk adjustment is implemented.
\end{cautionbox}

\medskip

\textbf{Implication:}

- Deaths averted from one disease do not reduce the at-risk population for the other
- Summing CVD + cancer results may overestimate total impact
- Acceptable for single-disease policy evaluation; requires adjustment for combined NCD investment cases

\medskip

\textbf{Planned for v2.0:} Shared population with competing-risk mortality across disease modules.

<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- PART 5 --- CLOSING                                            -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

# Part 5 --- Closing

## Summary

\begin{takeaway}[Summary]
\begin{itemize}
  \item Population-based discrete-time Markov framework for Indonesia NCD policy evaluation (2025--2050)
  \item CVD module: 4 causes, 4 intervention families, calibrated to GBD 2023
  \item Cancer module: 16 types in 3 tiers, screening interventions for Tier 3
  \item Economic evaluation via VSL/VSLY with income elasticity scenarios
  \item Agentic coding approach accelerates development while preserving auditability
\end{itemize}
\end{takeaway}

## Next Steps and Pending Work

\textbf{Near-term:}

- Finalize cancer intervention scenario definitions
- Generate publication-ready output figures for all modules
- Add \texttt{renv.lock} for dependency management
- Populate \texttt{docs/diagrams/} with model workflow visualizations

\medskip

\textbf{Medium-term:}

- Implement shared demography module
- Build formal integration layer (unified scenario config, joint output aggregation)
- Add competing-risk adjustment for combined NCD estimates

\medskip

\textbf{Longer-term:}

- Probabilistic sensitivity analysis (PSA)
- Extend to additional NCD domains (diabetes, COPD)
- Country adaptation toolkit for other LMICs

## Key References

\small

Core methodology and data:

- @Pickersgill2022 --- Global blood pressure target modeling
- @Kontis2019 --- Public health interventions and lives saved
- @Robinson2019VSL --- VSL methodology for global benefit-cost analysis
- @Watkins2025SPC --- Single-pill combination therapies impact

\medskip

Data sources:

- @Chong2024 --- Global CVD burden projections
- @Filippini2021 --- Sodium--blood pressure dose-response
- @Ettehad2016 --- Blood pressure lowering and CVD outcomes

## {.plain}

\vfill
\begin{center}
{\huge \textcolor{uwpurple}{Thank You}}

\bigskip

{\large Questions and Discussion}

\bigskip

\small
RTSL / University of Washington\\
Disease Control Priorities\\
\texttt{github.com/Disease-Control-Priorities}
\end{center}
\vfill

## References {.allowframebreaks}

\footnotesize
